model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: While Vanderbilt's elite offense (rank 6) and recent 91-74 SEC Tournament win over Florida present a compelling case, Florida's superior defensive efficiency (rank 5 vs 28) and higher overall KenPom/Torvik rankings (4 vs 12/10) provide a slight edge. The Gators' ability to control tempo and force turnovers could neutralize Vanderbilt's scoring, and their experience as a 1-seed in close tournament games gives them a narrow advantage despite the Commodores' recent head-to-head success.

reasoningStep.title: Florida vs Vanderbilt Sweet 16 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluating a matchup between two SEC teams with identical 26-7 records but contrasting strengths: Florida's elite defense versus Vanderbilt's elite offense.
evidence: Florida ranks #4 in both KenPom and Torvik with top-5 defensive efficiency (adjde rank 5) and top-11 offense (adjoe rank 11).
Vanderbilt ranks #12 KenPom/#10 Torvik with elite offense (rank 6) but weaker defense (rank 28).
Vanderbilt defeated Florida 91-74 in SEC Tournament semifinals on March 14, 2026, shooting 55% FG and 48% 3PT.
Florida won regular season matchup 98-94 on January 17, 2026, showing both teams can compete closely.
Both teams have identical 26-7 records and are familiar SEC opponents, reducing any surprise factor.
Florida's defensive prowess could disrupt Vanderbilt's offensive rhythm, while Vanderbilt's defensive vulnerabilities may be exploited by Florida's balanced attack.
